item.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

item.title: Last chance to apply — Startup Battlefield Australia applications close July 6
item.dc:creator: Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: If you're going to apply for Startup Battlefield Australia, now is the time. Applications close July 6, and once the deadline passes, the opportunity is gone.

item.title: IQM, Europe’s first public quantum company, admits the future of the tech is uncertain
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: IQM, a full-stack quantum company out of Finland, went public on the Nasdaq today at a valuation of about $1.9 billion.

item.title: Indian tech tycoon bets $30M of his own money to build AI alternative to Microsoft Office
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Neo is Bhavin Turakhia’s fifth venture and his latest involving enterprise software. This time he's taking on Microsoft Office and Google Apps with AI.

item.title: Bending Spoons defies SaaS slump, surges 40% on first day of trading
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The company has grown rapidly by acquiring and revamping last-generation tech brands like AOL, Eventbrite, Evernote, Meetup, and Vimeo.

item.title: Autonomous vehicle hype is back, and Humble Robotics is bringing it to freight
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: The autonomous vehicle space is starting to feel like a repeat of the 2016 hype cycle. Travis Kalanick is back building a robotics company, and the talent wars and capital are heating up the same way they did the first time around. The money’s flowing back, and it’s the people who lived through that first […]

item.title: Humble Robotics’ CEO says the tech finally caught up to the vision for autonomous vehicles
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec, Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: We’ve said it before, and we’ll say it again: the autonomous vehicle space is starting to feel like a repeat of the 2016 hype cycle. Travis Kalanick is back building a robotics company, and the talent wars and capital are heating up the same way they did the first time around. The money’s flowing back, and it’s the people who lived through that first wave […]

item.title: Builders Stage agenda revealed: Practical strategies for scaling startups at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: The Builders Stage is returning to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, bringing together 10,000+ founders, startup operators, and investors for practical conversations. and Q&A on what it takes to build and scale successful companies. Register now to save up to $330.

item.title: Startup Battlefield Australia application closes in days: Apply before July 6
item.dc:creator: Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: What if one pitch changed everything? The next company nobody has heard of yet is building something that will matter. It could be yours.

item.title: The DeepMind trio who built a poker AI are now making money for quant hedge funds
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: EquiLibre Technologies, a Prague-based AI lab founded by three ex-DeepMind researchers, is now valued at more than $500 million.

item.title: Clicks shows off its BlackBerry-inspired phone in a new hands-on video
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: A new video shows the final production version of the upcoming Clicks Communicator, a BlackBerry-like smartphone that runs modern apps.

item.title: Nvidia competitor Etched hits $5B valuation, $1B in sales for AI chip
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Nvidia AI chip competitor Etched says it has already booked $1 billion under contract for the inference systems powered by its chip.

item.title: Acti puts AI agents directly into your smartphone keyboard
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Acti is betting the smartphone keyboard is the next home for AI assistants. The startup's new keyboard for iOS and Android works across apps and lets users create custom AI-powered shortcuts using natural language.

item.title: Arcturus could halve the grid’s electrical losses using its nano-infused metals
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Stealthy startup Arcturus uses lasers to infuse carbon nanomaterials into copper, dramatically improving its ability to conduct electricity.

item.title: Your brand deserves its own stage — Side Events at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: From October 10-16, host a Side Event and command the room during the week of TechCrunch Disrupt 2026.

item.title: Chamath Palihapitiya raises $135M Series A for his AI coding startup, takes CEO role
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: VCs remain thirsty to fund AI coding startups. This one, founded by investor Chamath Palihapitiya, is no exception.

item.title: Arena, the AI leaderboard everyone uses, is now a $100M business
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The startup, which runs a popular free AI leaderboard, launched its commercial service just last September.

item.title: Omen AI’s plan to optimize data centers is all wet
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Omen AI raised a $31 million Series A to monitor chip coolant and stop bacterial outbreaks in data centers.

item.title: Asian AI startups launch Mythos-like models as Anthropic’s export ban drags on
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: New models are launching in Asia that promise Mythos-like capabilities without fear of an export ban. U.S. AI labs may never recover this enormous market.

item.title: Corgi, the buzzy Y Combinator-backed insurance tech startup, says it didn’t steal an open source product
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Corgi became embroiled in controversy when Papermark accused it of stealing its software. Corgi says it did not, raising new questions about vibe coding.
